summary: Closed, unmerged PR that decompiled three grmaterial functions with reported 100% fuzzy matches: grMaterial_801C9490, grMaterial_801C9604, and grMaterial_801C9698. The patch also corrected grMaterial_801C9470/grMaterial_801C9490 signatures from HSD_JObj* to Item_GObj*/CommandInfo* command-handler style and added the lb forward include for CommandInfo/ColorOverlay-related declarations. The author later noted that the remaining pointer arithmetic was intentional because the material system overlays ColorOverlay data into a shared Ground struct region that lacks proper typed fields, and the PR was eventually closed as consolidated into other PRs.

key_files.change: Replaced stubs for grMaterial_801C9490, grMaterial_801C9604, and grMaterial_801C9698 with matching C implementations; updated local prototypes and grMaterial_801C9470 forwarding signature.
Added lb/forward.h include and changed grMaterial_801C9470 prototype to take Item_GObj* and CommandInfo* instead of HSD_JObj*.

what_changed: Implemented grMaterial_801C9490 as an item command callback taking Item_GObj* and CommandInfo*, reading a u16 command argument from cmd->ptr[0], extracting an 8-bit value with shift/mask, storing it as f32 at Ground offset 0xC0, and setting gp->x10_flags.b6.
Changed grMaterial_801C9470 from an HSD_JObj* wrapper to a two-argument Item_GObj*/CommandInfo* wrapper that forwards directly to grMaterial_801C9490.
Implemented grMaterial_801C9604 to treat Ground offset 0x40 as a ColorOverlay, initialize priority, pointer, timer, loop, and color flags, clear gp->x10_flags.b4, then call grMaterial_801C9698.
Implemented grMaterial_801C9698 to call lb_80014258 with the gobj, the ColorOverlay overlay at Ground offset 0x40, and fn_801C9664, then set gp->x10_flags.b4 if the lb call succeeds.
Updated grmaterial.h to include lb/forward.h, needed by the revised declarations involving CommandInfo or lb-related forward types.
The PR body reported clean ninja verification and fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0 for all three decompiled functions.
The PR was closed without merge after the author said it was consolidated into other PRs.
smart_moves: Recognized that grMaterial_801C9490 is not a JObj routine but an item-command callback and corrected both the implementation signature and the public wrapper signature accordingly.
Used existing Ground flag bits, especially x10_flags.b4 and x10_flags.b6, instead of inventing new state names without structural evidence.
Kept explicit byte-offset casts for Ground offset 0x40 and 0xC0 where the current shared Ground struct cannot safely express the overlaid layout.
Connected grMaterial_801C9604 and grMaterial_801C9698 through the existing lb_80014258 ColorOverlay helper path instead of open-coding the overlay processing loop.

Documented why pointer math could not be trivially replaced with field access: ColorOverlay is overlaid into the Ground region around offset 0x40, and offset 0xC0 lies in unnamed padding before the GroundVars union.
decomp_lessons: Do not force struct field access when a game subsystem overlays unrelated data into a shared struct region; preserving byte-offset casts can be the most truthful and matching representation until the struct model is redesigned.

Ground object memory around offsets 0x40 through at least 0xC0 may be reused by material/color-overlay code rather than corresponding only to ordinary Ground fields such as self_vel, cur_pos, or padding.
A correct function signature can be a key matching tactic: changing grMaterial_801C9470/grMaterial_801C9490 from HSD_JObj* style to Item_GObj*/CommandInfo* style reflects their command-dispatch role.
When a proper type would require a non-trivial union in a widely shared struct, it is acceptable to leave localized pointer arithmetic and document the reason rather than destabilize unrelated code.
Closed/unmerged PRs can still contain useful matching evidence, but their contents should be cross-checked against the later consolidated PRs before reuse.
naming_conventions: Preserved address-derived names for still-unknown grmaterial routines: grMaterial_801C9490, grMaterial_801C9604, grMaterial_801C9698, fn_801C9664.
Used established project typedef names Item_GObj, HSD_GObj, Ground, CommandInfo, ColorOverlay, and ColorOverlay_x8_t.
Used existing Ground flag bitfield naming such as x10_flags.b4 and x10_flags.b6 rather than creating semantic names unsupported by the slice.
Kept unknown or partially understood fields in ColorOverlay form such as x4_pri, x8_ptr1, x0_timer, xC_loop, x7C_color_enable, and x7C_flag2.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Extracted a command value with (*(u16*) cmd->ptr[0] >> 2) & 0xFF, then converted it to f32 for storage, matching the apparent instruction behavior for grMaterial_801C9490.
Used byte-pointer arithmetic, (u8*) gp + 0xC0 and (u8*) gp + 0x40, to force exact offsets in the generated code.
Cast Ground offset 0x40 to ColorOverlay* for calls and field stores, matching a runtime overlay layout not represented in the current Ground struct.
Initialized ColorOverlay fields in grMaterial_801C9604 in a compact sequence before invoking grMaterial_801C9698, likely chosen to preserve store ordering and match codegen.
Used assignment chaining for co->x7C_color_enable = co->x7C_flag2 = 0, which may help reproduce the expected store pattern.
Set gp->x10_flags.b4 only after lb_80014258 returns true in grMaterial_801C9698, preserving the conditional side effect.

review_feedback.feedback: Author initially said they would fix pointer math soon.

Pointer math in grMaterial_801C9490 and grMaterial_801C9604/grMaterial_801C9698 could not be replaced with normal struct access because ColorOverlay is overlaid at Ground offset 0x40 and offset 0xC0 is in unnamed padding before GroundVars; a robust fix would need a union around Ground offsets 0x40-0xC3.
PR was consolidated into other PRs.
No formal review comments or reviews were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find the later PRs that consolidated grMaterial_801C9490, grMaterial_801C9604, and grMaterial_801C9698.
Search for any subsequent Ground struct union or layout change covering offsets 0x40 through 0xC3.
Check whether ColorOverlay overlay usage at Ground offset 0x40 appears in other stage or material files.
Verify whether grMaterial_801C9470 remains typed as Item_GObj*/CommandInfo* in the current tree.
Look for other lb_80014258 callers to infer stronger names for ColorOverlay fields and callback semantics.
